claims.claim_text: Knight Capital's August 1, 2012 deployment triggered dormant router logic, sent millions of erroneous orders, and caused a loss of more than $460 million in about 45 minutes.
claims.span_hint: section: Why This Module Matters, opening paragraph
claims.rationale: The SEC press release directly supports the timing, order volume, and loss figures, but sec.gov is off the allowlist.

claims.claim_text: `kubectl debug` supports creating workload copies, adding ephemeral containers to running Pods, and creating node debug Pods in host namespaces.
claims.span_hint: section: Cloud-Native Debugging with Kubernetes, first paragraph
claims.rationale: The kubectl reference lists those three supported debug actions explicitly.

claims.claim_text: Using `kubectl debug -f pod.yaml` to create an interactive debug session requires the `EphemeralContainers` feature to be enabled.
claims.span_hint: section: Cloud-Native Debugging with Kubernetes, ephemeral-container paragraph
claims.rationale: The kubectl debug reference includes this exact example and notes that it requires EphemeralContainers.

claims.claim_text: Debugging Kubernetes nodes with `kubectl` requires a cluster server version of at least 1.2.
claims.span_hint: section: Cloud-Native Debugging with Kubernetes, node-debug sentence
claims.rationale: The node-debug task page states that the Kubernetes server must be at or later than version 1.2.

claims.claim_text: Kubernetes v1.35 added structured, versioned JSON responses for z-pages such as `/statusz`, requested via Accept headers like `application/json;v=v1alpha1;g=config.k8s.io;as=Statusz`.
claims.span_hint: section: Cloud-Native Debugging with Kubernetes, z-pages paragraph
claims.rationale: The z-pages reference documents the v1.35 structured response behavior and the required Accept header.

claims.claim_text: `kubectl top` fetches metrics from Metrics Server, requires that component to be installed and running, and is meant as a low-overhead autoscaling signal rather than a full monitoring system.
claims.span_hint: section: Profiling Kubernetes Workloads, first two sentences
claims.rationale: The kubectl top reference says Metrics Server must be installed and frames the command as autoscaling-oriented rather than full monitoring.

claims.claim_text: Metrics Server is intended for built-in autoscaling use cases such as HPA and VPA, not as a general monitoring backend, and in its documented configuration it collects metrics every 15 seconds.
claims.span_hint: section: Profiling Kubernetes Workloads, metrics-server paragraph
claims.rationale: The Metrics Server README states both its autoscaling-only scope and its 15-second collection interval.

claims.claim_text: `kubectl top pod` metrics may be unavailable for a few minutes after Pod creation because of metrics pipeline delay.
claims.span_hint: section: Profiling Kubernetes Workloads, last sentence
claims.rationale: The kubectl top pod reference uses essentially this exact wording.

claims.claim_text: OpenAI strict-mode function calling relies on structured outputs and enforces schema adherence for tool arguments.
claims.span_hint: section: Debugging AI Tooling Integrations, strict-mode sentence
claims.rationale: The OpenAI docs state that setting `strict: true` leverages structured outputs to enforce the schema.

claims.claim_text: OpenAI announced in May 2025 that the Responses API gained remote MCP server support, image generation, Code Interpreter, and improved file search across GPT-4o, GPT-4.1, and o-series models.
claims.span_hint: section: Debugging AI Tooling Integrations, Responses API features paragraph
claims.rationale: The OpenAI product announcement directly lists those May 2025 Responses API additions and model families.

claims.claim_text: OpenAI hosts a public documentation MCP server at `https://developers.openai.com/mcp` that provides read-only access to developer docs.
claims.span_hint: section: Debugging AI Tooling Integrations, MCP docs sentence
claims.rationale: The Docs MCP page gives that server URL and describes it as read-only documentation access.

claims.claim_text: OpenAI web search in the Responses API supports non-reasoning and agentic modes, but is not available for `gpt-5` with minimal reasoning or for `gpt-4.1-nano`.
claims.span_hint: section: Debugging AI Tooling Integrations, web-search sentence
claims.rationale: The web search guide documents both the mode taxonomy and these model limitations.

claims.claim_text: The Morris Worm is presented with unsupported historical precision about primacy, damages, and reinfection behavior.
claims.span_hint: section: War Stories and Lessons, Morris Worm paragraph
claims.anchor_text: The Morris Worm was the first major internet worm, causing millions in damages. It possessed a fundamental design flaw regarding its probability of reinfection.
claims.suggested_rewrite: The Morris Worm showed how an aggressively self-replicating network worm could overload systems across the early internet.
claims.rationale: The sentence mixes disputed historical framing with unsourced damage claims, so the safe fix is to keep the lesson and drop the false precision.

claims.claim_text: The Grace Hopper anecdote incorrectly credits her with discovering the first literal computer bug and uses imprecise dating.
claims.anchor_text: Rear Admiral Grace Hopper discovered the first literal computer bug—a moth trapped in a relay of the Harvard Mark II—on September 9 of the late nineteen-forties.
claims.suggested_rewrite: One famous early computing anecdote involved a moth found in a relay of the Harvard Mark II, helping popularize the language of "bugs" and "debugging."
claims.rationale: The current wording overclaims Hopper's role and uses fuzzy dating for a specific historical anecdote.

claims.claim_text: The Therac-25 bullet overstates the causal chain by reducing the accidents to a single race condition.
claims.anchor_text: The Therac-25 radiation therapy machine delivered massive overdoses to patients between 1985 and 1987 due to a lethal race condition in its software.
claims.suggested_rewrite: The Therac-25 accidents between 1985 and 1987 showed how software defects and weak safety engineering in medical systems can lead to lethal overdoses.
claims.rationale: The historical record is more complex than a single race-condition explanation, so the statement should be narrowed to the supported lesson.

claims.claim_text: Knight Capital lost about $460 million in roughly 45 minutes on August 1, 2012 after a software deployment triggered dormant trading logic.
claims.rationale: The SEC press release supports the incident details, but sec.gov is not currently on the allowlist.

claims.claim_text: The Mars Climate Orbiter bullet overstates causality and includes an unsourced price figure.
claims.anchor_text: The Mars Climate Orbiter, an interplanetary probe costing $125 million, was completely destroyed on September 23, 1999, solely due to a metric-to-imperial unit conversion error.
claims.suggested_rewrite: The Mars Climate Orbiter was lost in 1999 after a navigation failure involving incompatible English and metric units, becoming a classic lesson in interface contracts and verification.
claims.rationale: NASA supports the units-mismatch lesson, but the current sentence adds unsupported cost precision and an overly absolute causal claim.

further_reading.title: Debug Running Pods
further_reading.why_relevant: Primary Kubernetes guidance for ephemeral containers, copied debug Pods, and node-level debugging workflows.

further_reading.title: OpenAI Function Calling Guide
further_reading.why_relevant: Primary OpenAI reference for tool-calling flow, strict mode, and schema requirements.

further_reading.title: Metrics Server
further_reading.why_relevant: Explains the resource-metrics pipeline behind `kubectl top`, including scope and collection interval.

notes: Excluded Mermaid diagrams, fenced code blocks, tables, and knowledge-check items per instructions. Historical prose in the 'Did You Know?' section was included.
